message:
chore(beads): update issue tracker — advance FUZZ-P2.4 to in-progress

Concurrent agent work updated bd-wz3gk (FUZZ-P2.4: Provider Stream
libfuzzer harnesses) from open to in_progress as work begins on
coverage-guided fuzzing for provider stream parsers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-wkdk","title":"Docs: keybindings.md (actions + config)","description":"# Goal
Create `docs/keybindings.md` documenting:
- supported actions
- default bindings
- customization via `~/.pi/agent/keybindings.json`

# Source Material
- Legacy: `legacy_pi_mono_code/pi-mono/packages/coding-agent/docs/keybindings.md`

# Must Include
- Key string format (`ctrl+shift+x`, etc.) and supported key names.
- Full action list grouped by category.
- Example configs (Emacs/Vim style).
- Note any Rust-specific differences (e.g., Windows Ctrl+Enter newline).

# Dependencies
- Should reflect implemented keybinding layer (`bd-3ip`).","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss

 {"id":"bd-ww5br","title":"[SEC-5.2] Trust onboarding wizard and emergency kill-switch workflow","description":"## Background
When risk rises, users need guided trust decisions and a one-step containment escape hatch.

## Scope
- Implement onboarding flow for quarantined/restricted extensions with clear risk disclosures.
- Implement emergency kill-switch to disable extension execution quickly.
- Record all decisions and kill-switch actions in audit logs.

## Deliverables
- Wizard flow spec and implementation.
- Kill-switch controls for interactive and headless environments.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Kill-switch reliably halts targeted extension activity.
- [ ] Onboarding requires explicit acknowledgment of risk level.
- [ ] Audit trail preserves decision provenance.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description is implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests added/updated for success, failure, edge cases, and determinism where applicable
[ ] E2E scripts added/updated for benign flow, adversarial flow, and rollback/recovery flow relevant to this bead
[ ] E2E runs emit structured JSONL logs with: timestamp, issue_id, extension_id, capability, policy_profile, score, reason_codes, action, latency_ms, correlation_id, and redaction_summary
[ ] Artifact manifest is deterministic and linked in bead comments (paths + checksums/identifiers)
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test (plus targeted conformance/security suites)
[ ] If runtime behavior changed, docs/config examples are updated; if no behavior changed, explicitly record N/A rationale for e2e changes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-14T04:39:42.346054870Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T11:44:12.027423290Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T11:44:00.000914887Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["incident-response","security","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ww5br","depends_on_id":"bd-21nj4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T04:40:01.409546025Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-ww5br","depends_on_id":"bd-qudx1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T04:40:01.635989322Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":1433,"issue_id":"bd-ww5br","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"TopazFalcon claiming bd-ww5br (SEC-5.2). Will implement: (1) kill-switch mechanism to immediately halt extension execution, (2) trust onboarding with explicit risk acknowledgment, (3) audit trail for all kill-switch and trust decisions.","created_at":"2026-02-14T11:08:29Z"},{"id":1435,"issue_id":"bd-ww5br","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"TopazFalcon completed SEC-5.2: Implemented kill-switch and trust onboarding. Added: ExtensionTrustState enum (Pending/Acknowledged/Trusted/Killed), kill_switch()/lift_kill_switch() methods on ExtensionManager with quarantine integration, record_trust_onboarding() with accept/reject flows, promote_trust() for trust lifecycle, KillSwitchAuditEntry and TrustOnboardingDecision audit types, 27 unit tests (all passing), 112 integration tests (all passing). Also cleaned up orphaned SEC-5.3 duplicate types from competing agents. Clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-02-14T11:44:12Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-wz3gk","title":"FUZZ-P2.4: Provider Stream libfuzzer harnesses — coverage-guided fuzzing for all providers","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-14T16:58:41.414296270Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T22:05:31.145571673Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fuzz","libfuzzer","providers"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-wz3gk","depends_on_id":"bd-291y7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T16:59:01.275183352Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":1480,"issue_id":"bd-wz3gk","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## FUZZ-P2.4: Provider Stream libfuzzer Harnesses

### Why This is P0 Priority
Provider stream parsers process UNTRUSTED API RESPONSES from external LLM services. A malicious or misconfigured API proxy could send crafted SSE events designed to crash the client. All 6+ providers have independent parsing code with independent bug potential.

### Harness Architecture
Create ONE parameterized harness that can target different providers:

```rust
// fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_provider_event.rs
#\![no_main]
use libfuzzer_sys::fuzz_target;
use arbitrary::Arbitrary;

#[derive(Arbitrary, Debug)]
struct FuzzInput {
    provider: u8,  // 0-5 maps to Anthropic/OpenAI/Gemini/Azure/Vertex/Bedrock
    event_type: String,
    event_data: String,
}

fuzz_target\!(|input: FuzzInput| {
    match input.provider % 6 {
        0 => fuzz_anthropic_event(&input.event_type, &input.event_data),
        1 => fuzz_openai_event(&input.event_type, &input.event_data),
        2 => fuzz_gemini_event(&input.event_type, &input.event_data),
        3 => fuzz_azure_event(&input.event_type, &input.event_data),
        4 => fuzz_vertex_event(&input.event_type, &input.event_data),
        _ => fuzz_bedrock_event(&input.event_type, &input.event_data),
    }
});
```

### Per-Provider Fuzz Functions
Each provider's process_event() takes (event_type, data_string) and returns StreamEvent. The fuzz function:
1. Calls process_event() with arbitrary type and data
2. Asserts no panic
3. For valid events, verifies output StreamEvent is well-formed

### Critical Code Paths Per Provider

**Anthropic**: 
- content_block_delta with tool_use type → accumulates JSON in current_tool_json
- index field → used as usize array index without bounds check
- usage.input_tokens/output_tokens → could be negative

**OpenAI**:
- choices[0].delta.tool_calls[0].function.arguments → deeply nested path
- finish_reason parsing → what if unknown string?
- usage object format differs from Anthropic

**Gemini**:
- candidates[0].content.parts[0] → deeply nested
- functionCall format → different JSON shape from OpenAI/Anthropic
- safetyRatings array → could be empty or malformed

### Seed Corpus
Extract event JSON from VCR cassettes:
- tests/fixtures/provider_responses/anthropic_stream.json
- tests/fixtures/provider_responses/openai_stream.json
- tests/fixtures/provider_responses/gemini_stream.json
- tests/fixtures/provider_responses/azure_stream.json
- Parse each cassette, extract individual SSE events as seed files

### Expected Findings
- Index out of bounds in Anthropic content block delta handling
- Panic on missing nested fields in Gemini response parsing
- Integer overflow in token count aggregation

### Files to Create
- fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_provider_event.rs
- fuzz/corpus/fuzz_provider_event/ (seed files from VCR cassettes)

### Acceptance Criteria
- Harness covers at least 3 providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini)
- Seed corpus includes real events from each provider's VCR cassettes
- 5-minute fuzz run per provider completes without crashes
- Any crashes → triaged, minimized, fixed","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:00:37Z"}]}

```rust
        0 => fuzz_anthropic_event(&input.event_type, &input.event_data),

```

1. Calls process_event() with arbitrary type and data
2. Asserts no panic
3. For valid events, verifies output StreamEvent is well-formed

### Critical Code Paths Per Provider

**Anthropic**: 
- content_block_delta with tool_use type → accumulates JSON in current_tool_json
- index field → used as usize array index without bounds check
- usage.input_tokens/output_tokens → could be negative

**OpenAI**:
- choices[0].delta.tool_calls[0].function.arguments → deeply nested path
- finish_reason parsing → what if unknown string?
- usage object format differs from Anthropic

**Gemini**:
- candidates[0].content.parts[0] → deeply nested
- functionCall format → different JSON shape from OpenAI/Anthropic
- safetyRatings array → could be empty or malformed

### Seed Corpus
Extract event JSON from VCR cassettes:
- tests/fixtures/provider_responses/anthropic_stream.json
- tests/fixtures/provider_responses/openai_stream.json
- tests/fixtures/provider_responses/gemini_stream.json
- tests/fixtures/provider_responses/azure_stream.json
- Parse each cassette, extract individual SSE events as seed files

### Expected Findings
- Index out of bounds in Anthropic content block delta handling
- Panic on missing nested fields in Gemini response parsing
- Integer overflow in token count aggregation

### Files to Create
- fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_provider_event.rs
- fuzz/corpus/fuzz_provider_event/ (seed files from VCR cassettes)

### Acceptance Criteria
- Harness covers at least 3 providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini)
- Seed corpus includes real events from each provider's VCR cassettes
- 5-minute fuzz run per provider completes without crashes
- Any crashes → triaged, minimized, fixed","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:00:37Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-wzzp4","title":"[SEC-4.2] Capability-scoped filesystem/network allowlists with least-privilege defaults","description":"## Background
Capability checks need concrete scope constraints (where, what, and how much), not just yes/no switches.

## Scope
- Define scope objects for file paths, network domains/endpoints, and connector classes.
- Enforce least-privilege defaults for new/unknown extensions.
- Add diagnostics for denied scope access with safe guidance.

## Deliverables
- Scope-aware policy structures and enforcement path.
- Fixtures for path traversal and egress abuse attempts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Scope checks block out-of-policy access deterministically.
- [ ] Defaults are restrictive yet usable for common safe extensions.
- [ ] Scope mismatch diagnostics are actionable.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description is implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests added/updated for success, failure, edge cases, and determinism where applicable
[ ] E2E scripts added/updated for benign flow, adversarial flow, and rollback/recovery flow relevant to this bead

[ ] Artifact manifest is deterministic and linked in bead comments (paths + checksums/identifiers)
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test (plus targeted conformance/security suites)
[ ] If runtime behavior changed, docs/config examples are updated; if no behavior changed, explicitly record N/A rationale for e2e changes","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"Codex","created_at":"2026-02-14T04:39:41.421403927Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T09:23:00.742921981Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T09:22:23.899453311Z","close_reason":"Implemented capability_manifest v2 connector_classes/hostcall_classes enforcement in wasm host dispatch path; added focused deny/allow tests; check+clippy passed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["containment","policy","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-wzzp4","depends_on_id":"bd-2ezm9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T04:39:59.320276246Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-wzzp4","depends_on_id":"bd-f0huc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T04:39:59.546614447Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":1369,"issue_id":"bd-wzzp4","author":"Codex","text":"Claiming bd-wzzp4 after bd-f0huc closure (selected by bv --robot-next).  {"id":"bd-x4ru","title":"Rustdoc: document core public types + add doc build check","description":"# Goal
Write rustdoc for the core public contract and add a lightweight CI check so docs don’t silently rot.

# Scope (recommended targets)
- `pi::model` (Message/ContentBlock/Usage/StreamEvent)
- `pi::provider` (Provider trait + StreamOptions)
- `pi::tools` (ToolRegistry + tool schemas)
- `pi::session` (Session + entry types)

# CI
- Add a CI step: `cargo doc --no-deps`.
- Optional: enable `#![warn(missing_docs)]` for the subset of modules we declare public (after the API surface audit).

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `cargo doc --no-deps` succeeds in CI.
- [ ] Core public types have meaningful docs (not tautologies).
- [ ] Any intentionally undocumented items are explicitly justified.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ChartreuseLake","created_at":"2026-02-03T21:24:08.685778173Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:28:50.367822108Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T09:03:05.839248953Z","close_reason":"Added rustdoc for model/provider/tools + CI cargo doc --no-deps","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-x4ru","depends_on_id":"bd-14od","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-x4ru","depends_on_id":"bd-3j4f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-x4x7","title":"Task: Implement agent lifecycle event dispatch (agent_start/agent_end)","description":"# Task: Implement Agent Lifecycle Event Dispatch

## Objective

Dispatch agent_start and agent_end events at the beginning and end of agent runs.

## Background

Agent lifecycle events allow extensions to:
- Initialize resources at agent start
- Clean up resources at agent end
- Log overall agent activity
- Modify system prompts (agent_start)

## TypeScript Reference

```typescript
// At start of agent.run()
if (runner.hasHandlers('agent_start')) {
    await runner.emit({
        type: 'agent_start',
        sessionId: session.id,
    });
}

// At end of agent.run() (including errors)
if (runner.hasHandlers('agent_end')) {
    await runner.emit({
        type: 'agent_end',
        sessionId: session.id,
        messages: conversation.messages,
        error: error?.message,
    });
}
```

## Implementation

### In Agent Run Method (src/agent.rs)

```rust
impl Agent {
    pub async fn run(&mut self) -> Result<AgentResult> {
        // Get event dispatcher if extensions are loaded
        let event_dispatcher = self.extension_manager
            .as_ref()
            .map(|em| em.event_dispatcher());
        
        // Dispatch agent_start
        if let Some(dispatcher) = &event_dispatcher {
            if dispatcher.has_handlers("agent_start") {
                let event = ExtensionEvent::AgentStart {
                    session_id: self.session.id().to_string(),
                };
                dispatcher.dispatch::<()>(event).await?;
            }
        }
        
        // Run agent loop
        let result = self.run_loop(&event_dispatcher).await;
        
        // Dispatch agent_end (even on error)
        if let Some(dispatcher) = &event_dispatcher {
            if dispatcher.has_handlers("agent_end") {
                let event = ExtensionEvent::AgentEnd {
                    session_id: self.session.id().to_string(),
                    messages: self.session.get_messages(),
                    error: result.as_ref().err().map(|e| e.to_string()),
                };
                let _ = dispatcher.dispatch::<()>(event).await;
                // Ignore dispatch errors during cleanup
            }
        }
        
        result
    }
}
```

## Event Data

### agent_start
```json
{
    "type": "agent_start",
    "sessionId": "abc-123"
}
```

### agent_end
```json
{
    "type": "agent_end",
    "sessionId": "abc-123",
    "messages": [...],
    "error": null
}
```

### agent_end (with error)
```json
{
    "type": "agent_end",
    "sessionId": "abc-123",
    "messages": [...],
    "error": "Rate limit exceeded"
}
```

## Handler Examples

```javascript
// Track agent sessions
ctx.on('agent_start', (event) => {
    analytics.track('agent_start', { sessionId: event.sessionId });
});

ctx.on('agent_end', (event) => {
    analytics.track('agent_end', {
        sessionId: event.sessionId,
        messageCount: event.messages.length,
        hadError: \!\!event.error,
    });
    
    if (event.error) {
        errorReporter.report(event.error);
    }
});
```

## Important Considerations

### agent_end Must Always Fire

Even if the agent loop errors or panics, agent_end should fire:

```rust
// Use a guard or finally pattern
struct AgentEndGuard<'a> {
    dispatcher: Option<&'a EventDispatcher>,
    session_id: String,
    fired: bool,
}

impl<'a> Drop for AgentEndGuard<'a> {
    fn drop(&mut self) {
        if \!self.fired {
            if let Some(dispatcher) = self.dispatcher {
                // Fire agent_end synchronously in drop
                // (or use a channel to signal)
            }
        }
    }
}
```

### Error Handling

- agent_start errors: Should abort the agent run
- agent_end errors: Should be logged but not propagate

## Testing

1. Unit test: agent_start fires before loop
2. Unit test: agent_end fires after successful run
3. Unit test: agent_end fires after error
4. Unit test: messages included in agent_end
5. Unit test: error included when agent fails
6. Integration test: Full lifecycle

## Dependencies

- Depends on: bd-tg4w (EventDispatcher)
- Part of: bd-10vp (Extension Event Hook Dispatch feature)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] agent_start dispatched at run start
- [ ] agent_end dispatched at run end
- [ ] agent_end fires even on error
- [ ] Event data correct
- [ ] Unit tests pass","status":"tombstone","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T20:12:02.982150788Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T21:11:16.534259181Z","source_repo":".","deleted_at":"2026-02-04T21:11:16.534251657Z","deleted_by":"ubuntu","delete_reason":"Merged into bd-13gm","original_type":"task","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
